prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `95755ff2`.
codex_response:
The rule for `95755ff2` is: keep the fixed 2-colored diamond, then extend every nonzero, non-2 border cell straight inward along its row or column, filling only zeros and stopping at the first existing nonzero blocker. I implemented that in [`verifier.py`](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_95755ff2/verifier.py#L4) and built a generator in [`generator.py`](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_95755ff2/generator.py#L125) that uses the same 11x11 diamond layout, contiguous opposite-side border runs, and sparse singleton/pair blockers inside active stripes, which matches the training family well.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint and discarded the Sonnet hint; the mismatch is recorded in [`notes.md`](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_95755ff2/notes.md#L1) because Sonnet described diagonal projection, while the official examples are orthogonal row/column fills. No new primitive was needed, so `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was unchanged. Official verification passed `4/4`, generated verification passed `1000/1000`, and the preview sheet looked on-distribution.
